message:
Propagate session header validation through session stack and prefer scanned metadata

Session Header Validation Propagation:
- session_index.rs: build_meta() and build_meta_from_jsonl() now call
  header.validate() and reject semantically invalid headers (wrong type,
  missing version/id/cwd) with descriptive Error::Session messages
- session_sqlite.rs: load_session(), load_session_meta(), and save_session()
  all validate headers before proceeding, catching malformed sessions at
  both read and write boundaries
- New test: build_meta_from_file_rejects_semantically_invalid_header()
- New SQLite tests: save/load reject headers with type != "session"

Session Picker Improvement:
- list_sessions_for_project() now unconditionally prefers disk-scanned metadata
  over cached index rows — disk scans are authoritative for successfully parsed
  session files, eliminating stale index entries (wrong message_count, size_bytes,
  or name) from appearing in the session list
- New test: list_sessions_for_project_prefers_scanned_meta_when_cached_row_is_stale()
  corrupts an index row and verifies the fresh scan overwrites it

Keybindings Test Addition:
- session_header_sync_ignores_incomplete_model_header_and_keeps_current_runtime():
  verifies that partial session headers (provider set, model_id missing) don't
  block runtime sync and thinking level is properly clamped to "off" for
  non-reasoning models

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
